nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires daily at 06:00 UTC. Adjust cron for other schedules (e.g. '0 */6 * * *' every 6 hours, '0 9 * * 1-5' weekdays at 9 AM).

nodes.name: Health Check
nodes.notes: GET /ready — Checks backend readiness (database + ML model). Uses continueOnFail so a connection error or 503 response flows into the IF node instead of crashing the workflow.

nodes.name: Is Backend Ready?
nodes.notes: TRUE when /ready returns {status:'ready'}. FALSE covers: backend down (network error), database unavailable (503), or ML model not loaded (503).

nodes.name: Run Analysis
nodes.notes: POST /api/v1/ml/run-analysis — Triggers background ML scoring. Returns {run_id, status:'running'}. Uses continueOnFail to catch 401 (expired JWT) or 403 (wrong role) gracefully.

nodes.name: Did Analysis Start?
nodes.notes: Checks for run_id in the response. FALSE means the POST failed (auth error, server error) — fires an alert instead of silently dying.

nodes.name: Init Poll State
nodes.notes: Initialises loop state: stores run_id and sets poll_count to 0. Subsequent iterations increment poll_count in 'Evaluate Status'.

nodes.name: Wait Before Poll
nodes.notes: 30-second pause between status polls. Loop ceiling: 20 iterations × 30s = 10 minutes max.

nodes.name: Check Run Status
nodes.notes: GET /api/v1/ml/run-status/{run_id} — Returns {run_id, status, transactions_scored, high_risk_count, duplicates_found, started_at, completed_at}.

nodes.name: Evaluate Status
nodes.notes: Merges HTTP response with loop state. Increments poll_count via self-reference (first pass defaults to 0). run_id is always read from Init Poll State to avoid drift.

nodes.name: Route by Status
nodes.notes: 3-way branch: output 0 = 'completed' → evaluate results. Output 1 = 'failed' → immediate error alert. Fallback output = still running → check retry limit.

nodes.name: Has Anomalies?
nodes.notes: Checks high_risk_count > 0 from the completed run.

nodes.name: Fetch High Risk Transactions
nodes.notes: GET /api/v1/dashboard/high-risk-transactions?limit=20 — Fetches top 20 high-risk transactions (v1 fetched default 100, wasting bandwidth). Returns: voucher_number, leak_probability, risk_factors, is_duplicate, duplicate_of.

nodes.name: Format Alert Message
nodes.notes: Builds a structured alert with run summary and top N high-risk transactions. Adds severity and source fields. Shows a truncation note when there are more HRTs than fetched.

nodes.name: Send Anomaly Alert
nodes.notes: Sends the anomaly alert to the configured webhook (Slack, Teams, Discord, or custom). Payload includes severity and source fields for downstream filtering.

nodes.name: Log Clean Run
nodes.notes: Analysis completed with zero high-risk transactions. No alert sent. This node exists as a clean exit point for audit visibility in the n8n execution log.

nodes.name: Send Run Failed Alert
nodes.notes: Fires immediately when the ML run reports status='failed'. No more polling — alerts ops to check backend logs.

nodes.name: Max Retries?
nodes.notes: Safety valve: poll_count >= 20 (10 minutes). TRUE → timeout alert. FALSE → loop back to Wait for another 30s cycle.

nodes.name: Send Timeout Alert
nodes.notes: Fires after 20 polls (10 min). Includes run_id and last known status so ops can investigate.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "text": "⚠️ *LeakWatch: Backend Unavailable*

The scheduled ML analysis was skipped because the backend health check failed.

Please verify:
• Backend is running at host.docker.internal:8000
• Database is accessible
• ML model is loaded

The workflow will retry on the next scheduled run.",
  "severity": "high",
  "source": "leakwatch"
}

nodes.name: Send Backend Down Alert
nodes.notes: Fires when /ready returns not_ready or is unreachable. Skips analysis entirely to avoid wasted work.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "text": "⚠️ *LeakWatch: Failed to Start Analysis*

The POST /api/v1/ml/run-analysis call failed.

Possible causes:
• Expired or invalid JWT token
• User does not have admin role
• Backend returned a server error

Please check the LEAKWATCH_JWT_TOKEN environment variable and backend logs.",
  "severity": "high",
  "source": "leakwatch"
}

nodes.name: Send Start Error Alert
nodes.notes: Fires when run-analysis returns an error (401, 403, 500, or network failure). Catches expired JWT tokens before entering the polling loop.
